features.description: Cargo workspace, TypeScript pnpm workspace, and uv Python environment are configured and all build targets compile cleanly.
Core type system defined in ucil-core/src/types.rs: QueryPlan, Symbol, Diagnostic, KnowledgeEntry, ToolGroup, CeqpParams, ResponseEnvelope.
ucil init command creates .ucil/ directory, detects project languages, and writes ucil.toml with sensible defaults.
ucil init --llm-provider prompts for LLM provider selection (ollama/claude/openai/passthrough/none) and writes [llm] section to ucil.toml.
ucil init runs P0 plugin health verification: checks that every P0-priority external tool binary is reachable and responds to a health probe.
ucil init writes .ucil/init_report.json with language detections, plugin health status, LLM provider, and schema version.
Schema version tracking in ucil-core: migration version stamped in .ucil/state.db, checked on daemon startup, triggers migration runner if behind.
CI pipeline configured: Rust cargo test + clippy, TypeScript biome check + vitest, Python ruff + mypy + pytest, all passing on a clean checkout.
OpenTelemetry skeleton wired in ucil-core/src/otel.rs: init_tracer() configures stdout exporter, span creation works, no panic on startup.
Full repository directory skeleton matches §17: crates/, adapters/, plugins/, ml/, tests/fixtures/, scripts/, docs/ all present and committed.
rust-project test fixture (~5K LOC Rust) committed to tests/fixtures/rust-project/ with Cargo.toml, src/, and tests/ directories.
python-project test fixture (~5K LOC Python) committed to tests/fixtures/python-project/ with pyproject.toml, src/, and tests/ directories.
typescript-project test fixture (~5K LOC TypeScript) committed to tests/fixtures/typescript-project/ with tsconfig.json, src/, and tests/ directories.
mixed-project test fixture (~3K LOC with intentional lint errors, type errors, security issues, and failing tests) committed to tests/fixtures/mixed-project/.
ucil-treesitter crate implements multi-language parser using tree-sitter Rust bindings supporting ≥10 languages including Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, and Java.
Symbol extraction from tree-sitter ASTs yields function, class, method, struct, enum, and trait definitions with line ranges and signatures.
AST-aware chunking splits source files at function/class boundaries into ≤512-token chunks suitable for embedding.
LMDB tag cache (via heed crate) maps (file_path, mtime) → Vec<Symbol>; warm reads complete in <1ms on fixture rust-project.
Session manager creates per-agent sessions with isolated state, detects current git branch, and discovers worktree paths.
Two-tier storage layout initialised: .ucil/shared/ for cross-branch data and .ucil/branches/<branch>/ for per-branch symbol indexes.
Daemon process lifecycle: daemonize with PID file at .ucil/daemon.pid, clean shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGHUP, PID file removed on exit.
File watcher using notify + notify-debouncer-full: 100ms debounce for editor events, PostToolUse hook path bypasses debounce immediately.
Watchman integration: auto-detected at ucil init for repos >50K files; persistent subscriptions and cookie-based sync; fallback to PollWatcher for network mounts.
Salsa incremental engine skeleton: dependency DAG tracks computations, invalidation propagates on file change, early cutoff stops propagation when result unchanged.
Plugin manager skeleton: discovers plugin.toml manifests, spawns plugin processes, performs health checks via tools/list MCP call.
HOT/COLD plugin lifecycle: plugins with hot_cold=true auto-transition to IDLE after idle_timeout_minutes, restart on next relevant query.
Basic MCP server over stdio: all 22 UCIL tools registered with schemas; unimplemented tools return {_meta:{not_yet_implemented:true}}.
Progressive startup sequence: MCP server accepts queries within 2 seconds of daemon start; priority indexing queue serves recently-queried files first.
Crash recovery: daemon stores last-good checkpoint in .ucil/checkpoint.json; on restart it resumes indexing from the checkpoint without full re-index.
SQLite schema created with WAL mode, PRAGMA busy_timeout=10000, BEGIN IMMEDIATE writes: tables for symbols, knowledge_entries, hot_staging, warm, quality_issues, feedback, conventions, sessions.
knowledge_graph.rs: CRUD operations for KnowledgeEntry with bi-temporal queries (valid_time, transaction_time), entity resolution, and provenance tracking.
Symbol resolution: given symbol name + optional file scope, returns definition location, signature, docstring, and parent module from the knowledge graph.
Tree-sitter extraction pipeline populates knowledge graph: file change → parse → extract symbols → upsert to KG with WAL transaction batching.
find_definition MCP tool returns definition location, signature, hover doc, and immediate callers from tree-sitter + KG for a symbol in the fixture rust-project.
CEQP universal parameters wired on all 22 tool schemas: reason, current_task, files_in_context, token_budget accepted on every tool call.
Session state tracking: per-agent session stores call history, inferred domain, files_in_context set, branch, and worktree path with TTL expiry.
Hot staging writes: all knowledge graph writes go through hot_staging table first using BEGIN IMMEDIATE; WAL checkpoint triggered at configurable interval.
understand_code MCP tool returns a structured explanation of a file, function, or module via tree-sitter AST summary + knowledge graph facts.
get_conventions MCP tool returns project-specific coding conventions from the conventions table, empty list if none yet extracted.
Serena plugin manifest (plugin.toml) written; ucil plugin install serena succeeds; MCP client sends tools/list and receives ≥10 Serena tools.
Serena wired into G1 structural group: find_symbol, find_references, and go_to_definition results enriched with Serena hover info for the fixture projects.
ucil-lsp-diagnostics crate implements LspDiagnosticsBridge: connects to Serena-managed LSP server instances via shared socket; no duplicate LSP processes when Serena active.
LSP JSON-RPC client implements textDocument/diagnostic, callHierarchy/incomingCalls, callHierarchy/outgoingCalls, and typeHierarchy/supertypes requests.
Diagnostics bridge feeds G7 quality pipeline: type errors and lint warnings for fixture python-project appear in quality_issues table within 5s of daemon start.
Diagnostics bridge feeds G4 architecture: call hierarchy (incoming/outgoing calls) and type hierarchy (supertypes) enriched for symbols in fixture projects.
Degraded mode: when Serena is unavailable, bridge spawns its own pyright, rust-analyzer, and typescript-language-server processes with 5-minute grace period shutdown.
LSP and Serena integration test suite (tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs) passes against all four fixture projects: type errors detected, call hierarchies populated.
search_code MCP tool returns results from tree-sitter symbol index and ripgrep text search merged and de-duplicated.
Plugin manifest parser: reads plugin.toml, validates all required fields (name, version, capabilities, transport), extracts language activation rules.
Plugin lifecycle manager: full state machine (DISCOVERED→REGISTERED→LOADING→ACTIVE→IDLE→STOPPED→ERROR) with monitored transitions and error logging.
Plugin hot-reload: ucil plugin reload <name> restarts the plugin process without restarting the daemon; active queries complete before reload.
Circuit breakers for plugin crashes: after max_restarts=3 failures the plugin enters ERROR state; exponential backoff (base 1s) between restart attempts.
ast-grep plugin manifest written; ast-grep binary installed; MCP server starts and responds to a structural pattern search on the fixture typescript-project.
Probe plugin manifest written; probe binary installed; MCP server returns complete function bodies with token budgets from the fixture rust-project.
ucil plugin CLI subcommands (list, install, uninstall, enable, disable, reload) all work and produce machine-readable JSON output with --format json.
Plugin lifecycle integration test suite (tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs) verifies HOT/COLD transitions, crash recovery, and circuit breaker behaviour.
G1 parallel execution: on a structural query, tree-sitter, Serena, ast-grep, and the diagnostics bridge all run concurrently with tokio::join! and a 5s timeout.
G1 result fusion: results merged by source location (file + line range), unique information unioned, conflict resolved by source authority (Serena > tree-sitter > ast-grep).
G2 intra-group RRF fusion: Probe×2.0, ripgrep×1.5, LanceDB×1.5 weights applied in the Reciprocal Rank Fusion formula with k=60.
Session deduplication: files already in files_in_context are excluded from search results; session-scoped dedup store is cleared on session expiry.
find_references MCP tool returns all references to a symbol grouped by usage type (call, import, type-annotation) from fused G1/G2 results.
search_code MCP tool returns fused G2 results (ripgrep text + Probe AST-aware + LanceDB semantic) ranked by weighted RRF.
ripgrep plugin configuration and smoke test: ripgrep binary in PATH, searches fixture projects respecting .gitignore, returns structured match results.
SCIP P1 install: scip-rust and scip CLI produce a cross-repo symbol index for the fixture rust-project; index loaded into SQLite and queried via G1.
LanceDB per-branch vector store lifecycle: new branch → new LanceDB table created; branch deletion → table archived; delta indexing from parent branch on creation.
ucil-embeddings crate wraps ONNX Runtime via ort crate; initialises session from a local .onnx model file; infer() accepts token IDs and returns embedding vectors.
CodeRankEmbed 137M model (Int8 quantized, ~137MB): downloaded to ml/models/, loaded via ort, produces 768-dim embeddings for code snippets.
Qwen3-Embedding 8B GPU upgrade path: config-gated via ucil.toml embedding_model=qwen3-embedding; Matryoshka dimension support (32–7168); skipped if no GPU detected.
LanceDB background chunk indexing: file changes trigger async embedding of new/changed chunks; incremental updates skip unchanged chunks via mtime check.
Embedding chunker: splits source files at tree-sitter function/class boundaries into chunks of ≤512 tokens; preserves file path and line-range metadata.
Embedding throughput benchmark: CodeRankEmbed produces ≥50 embeddings/second on CPU; measured by criterion on 100 fixture code snippets.
Vector query latency benchmark: p95 warm LanceDB semantic search on pre-indexed fixture rust-project completes in <100ms.
find_similar MCP tool returns semantically similar code snippets via LanceDB vector search using CodeRankEmbed query embeddings.
Deterministic query classifier: maps (tool_name, reason_keywords) → QueryType with intent, domain tags, and group weight overrides; used as fallback when provider=none.
CEQP reason parser: extracts intent (add_feature/fix_bug/refactor/understand/review), domains, planned_action, and knowledge_gaps from the free-text reason parameter.
Parallel executor: fans out queries to all relevant tool groups concurrently via tokio::join_all; per-group 5s timeout returns empty results + _meta.degraded_groups on timeout.
Cross-group RRF fusion engine: implements Σ(w_g × 1/(k+rank_g(d))) with k=60 using the 10×8 query-type weight matrix from §6.2.
Codebase-Memory MCP plugin installed; MCP client sends a symbol lookup query and receives structured knowledge graph results.
Mem0 plugin installed; Python SDK available; agent memory CRUD (store observation, retrieve by key, list recent) works via MCP.
G3 (Knowledge) parallel query: Codebase-Memory MCP and Mem0 queried concurrently; results merged by entity with temporal priority (newest wins on conflict).
CodeGraphContext plugin installed; blast radius query for a function in the fixture rust-project returns affected files and dependency chains.
G4 (Architecture) parallel query: CodeGraphContext blast radius + LSP call hierarchy combined; union of all dependency edges computed.
Graphiti P1 plugin installed; bi-temporal knowledge graph query returns facts with validity windows; P95 ≤300ms verified on fixture data.
Incremental computation integration test suite (tests/integration/test_incremental.rs): whitespace-only file change triggers early cutoff and does not re-run symbol extraction.
Aider repo-map reimplemented in Rust: PageRank over call graph with 50× relevance bias for recently-queried files; fits symbol list into a configurable token budget.
Context7 plugin installed; real-time docs for ≥9000 libraries accessible; query for a library used in the fixture typescript-project returns current API docs.
Repomix plugin installed; repo-to-context packing of fixture rust-project completes in <5s and produces ≥60% token reduction vs naive file concatenation.
G5 (Context) parallel query: Aider repo-map + Context7 + Repomix run concurrently; results ranked by PageRank relevance; session-deduped against files_in_context.
GitHub MCP server installed; repository file list, PR list, and Actions workflow status queries succeed against a real or mocked GitHub API.
Git MCP server installed; git log, git blame, and git diff queries succeed on the fixture rust-project repository.
Filesystem MCP server installed; secure file read and directory listing work within configured allowed paths.
G6 (Platform) aggregation layer: Git MCP + GitHub MCP + Filesystem MCP run concurrently and results merged into a unified platform context block.
Quality-maximalist response assembly: session dedup applied, results below relevance threshold excluded (score<0.1), remaining content included at full detail, _meta.token_count annotated.
Conflict resolution: source authority hierarchy (LSP/AST > SCIP > KG > text) as soft guidance; unresolvable conflicts returned with confidence metadata.
Bonus context selector: conventions, pitfalls, quality_issues, related_code, tests_to_update, blast_radius, history, and security entries attached when relevance score ≥0.1.
Multi-tier query merging: hot_staging, warm, and cold tier results merged into a single response with tier provenance metadata.
Warm processors: four rule-based processors (observation: 60s, convention_signal: 60s, architecture_delta: 120s, decision_linker: 60s) promote hot data to warm tier on schedule.
dependency-cruiser P0 (JS/TS) plugin installed; module rule validation on the fixture typescript-project detects a circular dependency.
Zoekt P1 plugin installed; trigram index built over the fixture projects; multi-file search query returns results faster than ripgrep on warm index.
get_architecture MCP tool returns a structured architecture overview: module boundaries, key components, and data flow from G4 blast radius + CodeGraphContext.
trace_dependencies MCP tool returns upstream and downstream dependency chains for a file/module/symbol via G4 analysis with depth configurable.
blast_radius MCP tool returns impacted files from a hypothetical change using BFS over the dependency graph weighted by coupling strength.
G7 quality pipeline entry: LSP diagnostics bridge registered as the always-available G7 source; type errors and lint warnings appear in every check_quality response.
ESLint built-in MCP plugin installed; JS/TS lint errors from the fixture typescript-project returned with file, line, rule, and severity.
Ruff MCP plugin installed; Python lint errors and format violations from the fixture python-project returned with file, line, rule, and fix suggestion.
Semgrep MCP plugin installed; SAST scan of the fixture mixed-project detects ≥1 security finding using the OWASP rule set.
G7 severity-weighted merge fusion: issues from all G7 tools merged by severity (critical>high>medium>low), deduplicated by (file, line, category), highest severity kept.
quality_issues table tracking: first_seen, last_seen, resolved timestamps updated per-issue; resolved issues soft-deleted after 7 days; trend metrics queryable.
test-runner-mcp plugin installed; unified test execution covers Rust (cargo nextest), Python (pytest), and TypeScript (vitest) via MCP protocol.
mcp-pytest-runner plugin installed; pytest hierarchical test discovery and selective re-run by node ID verified on the fixture python-project.
G8 test discovery uses all three methods concurrently: convention-based (src/foo.rs → tests/test_foo.rs), import-based (which test files import changed module), and KG tested_by relations.
check_quality MCP tool routes to G7+G8, returning issues[] with severity/category/file/line/fix_suggestion and untested_functions[] for changed code.
review_changes MCP tool analyses a diff against conventions, quality, security, test coverage, and blast radius; returns structured review with severity-ranked findings.
Feedback loop post-hoc analyser: after each response, tracks which bonus context categories were used by the agent (via subsequent calls); boosts/decays importance scores.
Quality pipeline integration test suite (tests/integration/test_quality_pipeline.rs): runs against mixed-project fixture and asserts ≥3 severity-classified issues detected.
Testing pipeline integration test suite (tests/integration/test_testing_pipeline.rs): test discovery finds all tests in fixture projects; run results and coverage returned.
type_check MCP tool returns type error diagnostics for specified files via the LSP diagnostics bridge, covering at least Python, TypeScript, and Rust.
Full query pipeline integration test suite (tests/integration/test_query_pipeline.rs + test_fusion.rs): golden queries assert correct fused results with expected group provenance.
LlmProvider trait defined in ucil-agents/src/provider.rs: async complete(), is_available(), cost_per_1k_tokens(), max_context_tokens(), name(); all providers implement it.
Ollama local provider: connects to http://localhost:11434, sends chat completion request, handles streaming response, 10s timeout per request.
Claude API provider: calls Anthropic API with prompt caching enabled, streaming support, configurable model, 15s timeout.
OpenAI API provider: calls OpenAI completions API with streaming support, configurable model (default gpt-4o), 15s timeout.
Host passthrough provider: embeds synthesis instructions and key facts in response _synthesis_hint field; is_available() always true; zero LLM calls.
None (deterministic) provider: is_available() returns false; triggers keyword-based classifier and source-authority conflict resolution; no LLM calls made.
Query Interpreter agent: analyses reason+target+session context via LLM to produce QueryPlan with intent, domains, sub-queries, and knowledge_gaps; runs on every query.
Synthesis Agent: takes fused group results and produces a coherent narrative via LLM; in host-passthrough mode embeds instructions in _synthesis_hint.
Agent framework smoke test: Interpreter and Synthesis agents both produce non-empty output for a find_definition query against fixture rust-project with provider=ollama.
Conflict Mediator agent: detects disagreements between group outputs, reasons about them using LLM with source authority as soft guidance, returns resolution with confidence.
Clarification Agent: uses MCP Elicitation protocol to ask user for clarification when query intent is genuinely ambiguous; ≤2 clarifications per query.
Background agent scheduler: async task queue dispatches Convention Extractor, Memory Curator, and Architecture Narrator on their configured schedules without blocking query-path.
Convention Extractor background agent: samples 50-100 files every 10 commits, sends to LLM to discover coding patterns, stores to conventions table with evidence counts.
Memory Curator background agent: deduplicates and merges agent observations every 24h and after each session; clusters by semantic similarity; prunes low-importance entries.
Architecture Narrator background agent: produces ≤2000-token architectural narrative every 50 commits; stored in knowledge graph with generation timestamp.
Full agent integration test suite (tests/integration/test_multi_agent.rs): 5 concurrent agent sessions run without data races; branch isolation verified; shared knowledge propagated.
CEQP integration test suite (tests/integration/test_ceqp.rs): golden queries with rich reason strings assert expected bonus context categories are populated.
Resilience integration test suite skeleton (tests/integration/test_resilience.rs): fault injection framework scaffolded; plugin crash and DB corruption cases as pending tests.
Claude Code plugin package built: plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json and plugin/.mcp.json wired to ucil-mcp server; plugin installs cleanly via ucil init --install-claude-plugin.
PostToolUse hook (plugin/hooks/post-write-analyze.py) notifies ucild of file changes immediately, bypassing the 100ms notify debounce window.
Skills: /ucil-lint skill invokes check_quality MCP tool and displays a structured lint report with file, line, severity, and fix suggestion.
Skills: /ucil-security skill invokes security_scan MCP tool and displays security findings with CVE references and remediation guidance.
Skills: /ucil-test skill invokes run_tests MCP tool and displays test results with pass/fail counts, duration, and coverage delta.
Skills: /ucil-review skill invokes review_changes MCP tool on the current git diff and displays a structured code review.
ucil-lint.md subagent (haiku model) defined: lint-focused agent that calls check_quality, interprets results, and proposes fixes in concise form.
ucil-security.md subagent (sonnet model) defined: security-focused agent that calls security_scan, ranks findings by exploitability, and drafts remediation PRs.
ucil-test.md subagent defined: test discovery + execution agent that calls run_tests, analyses failures, and suggests fixes.
ucil-review.md subagent defined: code review agent that calls review_changes and get_conventions, then produces a structured PR review comment.
ucil init --install-claude-plugin copies the plugin package to ~/.claude/plugins/ucil/ and writes .mcp.json entry; subsequent claude --tools list shows ucil-mcp.
Codex CLI adapter: generates AGENTS.md with UCIL context, applies aggressive compression for 10KB tool output limit, paginates large results with continuation tokens.
Cursor adapter: writes .cursor/mcp.json pointing to ucil-mcp server; exposes core 5 tools (understand_code, find_definition, search_code, get_context_for_edit, blast_radius).
Cline/Roo Code adapter: generates cline_mcp_settings.json with mode-specific tool filtering and alwaysAllow list; full response format preserved.
Aider adapter: HTTP bridge (FastAPI) exposes UCIL tools over REST; enhanced repo-map embedded in Aider's --map-tokens context.
Ollama adapter: aggressive compression applied (signature-only mode for code blocks, 4K-32K context window respected, no bonus context for simple queries).
Auto-detect adapter: detects host environment from env vars (CLAUDE_MCP_SERVER, CURSOR_EXTENSION_HOST, CLINE_MCP, AIDER_MODEL) and applies the correct adapter.
Playwright MCP P1 plugin installed; browser automation via accessibility tree works; a page title extraction smoke test passes.
Host adapter integration test suite (tests/integration/test_host_adapters.rs): each adapter applies correct response transformation for its host's constraints.
Git post-commit hook integration: ucild re-indexes changed files within 2s of a commit; KG updated with new commit SHA as provenance.
PR context extraction: on branch merge or PR event (via GitHub MCP), PR title, description, review comments, and diff summary stored as KG history entries.
remember MCP tool full implementation: stores agent observations, decisions, and learnings to Mem0 with three-tier scoping (user/session/agent); retrieves by semantic similarity.
Snyk MCP P1 plugin installed; SCA dependency vulnerability scan of the fixture python-project returns ≥0 CVEs from Snyk database.
OSV MCP P1 plugin installed; CVE database query for npm, PyPI, Go, and Maven ecosystems returns structured vulnerability data.
TruffleHog installed; secrets detection scan of the fixture mixed-project detects the intentional test API key with live verification disabled.
Gitleaks installed; git history scan of the fixture mixed-project repository detects the intentional test secret in commit history.
Security tools wired into G7 fusion: Snyk + OSV + TruffleHog + Gitleaks results severity-merged with existing LSP and SAST findings.
security_scan MCP tool full implementation: routes to G7 SAST+SCA+secrets+container pipeline, returns findings[] with severity/CVE/remediation sorted by exploitability.
lint_code MCP tool full implementation: routes to G7 language-specific linters (ESLint/Ruff/cargo-clippy), returns lint issues[] with fix suggestions.
Coverage analysis wired into G8: lcov and cobertura report parsing extracts per-file line and branch coverage; changed-file coverage delta included in G8 results.
Importance decay rules for cold tier: entity_decay=0.99, observation_decay=0.95, decision_decay=0.999 applied per compaction cycle; entries below min_importance=0.05 pruned.
Periodic compaction: stale KG nodes pruned, invalid relations removed, resolved quality_issues soft-deleted, triggered every 6 hours and on explicit ucil compact.
Tiered GC: hot staging entries older than 60 minutes deleted; warm entries older than 7 days and not promoted deleted; promoted entries kept 24 hours after promotion.
Background agents consume warm tier data: Convention Extractor and Memory Curator read from warm tier (faster, cheaper) rather than querying all live sources.
Arc Memory P1 plugin installed; git-native knowledge graph built from fixture rust-project; ADRs and commit messages linked to code symbols via MCP query.
explain_history MCP tool: returns why code was written (PR/issue/ADR context) via Arc Memory G6 git integration; response includes linked PR descriptions and author rationale.
SonarQube MCP P1 plugin installed; code quality metrics for the fixture rust-project returned (code smells, duplications, coverage); 19+ language support confirmed.
Trivy P1 plugin installed; container image and filesystem vulnerability scan of fixture projects produces SARIF-format output; wired into G7 severity merge.
Open Context P1 plugin installed; Go, npm, Python, Rust, Docker, K8s, Terraform docs retrieved for symbols in fixture projects via MCP.
Axon P1 plugin installed; dependency analysis, call chains, and module clusters generated for the fixture typescript-project; wired into G4 parallel query.
Knowledge tiering integration test suite (tests/integration/test_knowledge_tiering.rs): timed assertions verify hot→warm→cold promotion and GC within configured time windows.
Cross-project knowledge groups: ~/.ucil/groups/ stores group metadata and shared conventions; per-project group membership persisted in ucil.toml.
Cross-project CLI commands: ucil group create/join/leave/list/sync all functional; sync propagates group conventions to project knowledge graph.
Convention promotion pipeline: conventions seen in ≥3 projects within a group auto-promoted to group scope; ucil convention promote <id> allows manual promotion.
review_changes MCP tool full implementation: complete diff analysis routing to G1/G4/G7/G8 for conventions, quality, security, test coverage, and blast radius.
generate_docs MCP tool: generates architecture overview, module-level docs, API reference, and onboarding guide from G3+G4+G5 data using the Synthesis agent.
pytest-mcp P1 plugin installed; pytest plugin exposes test results as MCP resources; selective re-run by node ID verified on the fixture python-project.
deptry P1 (Python) plugin installed; Python dependency hygiene check on the fixture python-project detects unused and missing transitive dependencies.
Cross-project integration test suite (tests/integration/test_cross_project.rs): multi-project fixture verifies convention sync, promotion, and group-scoped knowledge.
Feedback loop integration test suite (tests/integration/test_feedback_loop.rs): simulated agent sequences verify importance boost/decay from bonus context usage tracking.
L0 in-memory LRU cache: content-addressed hash of (query_params + file_state_hash) as key; max 1000 entries; cache hit measured at <1ms on identical queries.
L1 LMDB cache: serialised query results persisted in LMDB; max 100MB; TTL=300s; hit measured at <10ms; invalidated by Salsa dependency graph change.
L2 full invocation cache: per-tool result cached keyed by tool_name + normalised query + plugin versions; TTL=300s; saves 100ms-seconds on repeated expensive plugin calls.
Cache invalidation tied to Salsa: when a file change invalidates a Salsa query, all cache tiers (L0/L1/L2) for dependent results are evicted atomically.
OTel full span hierarchy: all spans from §15.2 implemented with correct parent-child relationships, span names matching ucil.<layer>.<operation> convention.
OTel metrics: all 11 metrics from §15.3 exported (query duration histogram, cache hit rate gauge, group duration histogram, plugin state gauge, etc.).
Jaeger export via OTLP gRPC: configurable endpoint (default localhost:4317), configurable sample_rate; spans reach Jaeger on a local test instance.
Resource limits: RSS monitoring warns at 512MB and throttles background indexing at 640MB; disk usage tracking warns at 80% of configured max (2048MB); LRU eviction on pressure.
Indexing benchmark suite (benches/bench_indexing.rs): criterion measures initial index time and incremental update time for fixture rust-project.
Query latency benchmark: p95 for all 22 MCP tools under sustained load is <500ms; measured by criterion against fixture projects with warm cache.
Token efficiency benchmark: UCIL enriched response provides ≥2× more unique relevant information per output token than naive ripgrep + file-cat baseline.
Memory usage benchmark: daemon RSS remains <512MB under a sustained 1-hour load test with 5 concurrent agent sessions and background indexing active.
Cache hit rate benchmark: after 10-minute warm-up, L0 hit rate ≥70% and L1 hit rate ≥85% for a realistic query replay trace.
Multi-agent contention benchmark: 5 concurrent agent sessions achieve ≥50% of single-agent throughput with p99 latency <2000ms.
Embedding throughput benchmark phase-6: CodeRankEmbed ≥50 embeddings/sec CPU, Qwen3-Embedding ≥10 embeddings/sec GPU (if GPU available), measured with criterion.
Serena + LSP diagnostics bridge latency benchmark: p95 for textDocument/diagnostic fetch per language is <500ms on a warm LSP server.
Benchmark regression CI: compares current benchmark results against stored baseline; fails CI if any p95 metric regresses by >20%.
docs/benchmarks.md: full benchmark report with methodology, hardware environment, results tables for all benchmarks, and comparison baseline.
OTel observability integration test suite (tests/integration/test_otel.rs): span assertion library verifies correct span hierarchy, names, attributes, and metric values.

Resilience integration test suite completion (tests/integration/test_resilience.rs): plugin crash recovery, SQLite corruption handling, missing dependency graceful degradation, and progressive startup all pass.
DBHub MCP plugin installed; universal database schema access returns table definitions and relationships for a test SQLite database.
Prisma MCP plugin installed; migration management queries return pending migration list and schema diff for a Prisma-based fixture project.
Database tools wired into G5 (schema as context) and G4 (database entity relationships in architecture graph): DBHub and Prisma MCP results integrated.
query_database MCP tool full implementation: schema inspection, migration status, and query analysis routed to G5/G4 database tools.
Docker MCP P1 plugin installed; container list, Dockerfile analysis, and image inspection queries succeed; three-tier safety model verified.
Terraform MCP plugin installed; IaC provider docs and module search queries succeed for a fixture Terraform project.
Sentry MCP plugin installed; runtime error query, trace lookup, and performance data retrieval succeed against a Sentry test project.
check_runtime MCP tool full implementation: queries Sentry/Datadog for errors, traces, and performance data for the current project via G6 platform tools.
GitNexus plugin installed; blast radius and git-diff impact mapping for the fixture rust-project returns affected modules; registered as G4 alternative to CodeGraphContext.
refactor MCP tool: safe cross-file refactoring using Serena replace_symbol_body for body replacement and find_references for call-site updates; atomic rollback on failure.
docs/architecture.md written: full architecture documentation covering all 7 layers, 8 tool groups, query pipeline, agent layer, and storage schema with diagrams.
docs/plugin-development.md written: plugin.toml schema reference, lifecycle state machine, activation rules, health check protocol, and two worked examples.
docs/host-adapter-guide.md written: per-host installation and configuration guide for Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor, Cline, Aider, and Ollama.
docs/configuration.md written: complete ucil.toml reference documenting all configuration keys with types, defaults, and examples.
docs/observability.md written: OTel span hierarchy reference, metrics catalogue, Jaeger setup instructions, and MCPcat integration guide.
docs/serena-diagnostics-guide.md written: Serena + LSP diagnostics bridge setup, language coverage table, degraded mode behaviour, and troubleshooting FAQ.
docs/claude-code-integration.md written: Claude Code plugin install guide, skill and hook reference, subagent descriptions, and example session transcripts.
docs/benchmarks.md final: updated benchmark report with production results, environment details, methodology, and comparison against Aider and Cursor baselines.
Schema migration system: versioned SQL migration runner with auto-backup before each migration; migration history tracked in schema_version table.
ucil export-brain command: exports knowledge.db, memory.db, history.db, ucil.toml, and plugin manifests to a tar.gz archive; excludes branch indexes and session data.
ucil import-brain command: merge mode deduplicates entities and observations; replace mode overwrites shared knowledge; both modes preserve local branch indexes.
ucil backup / ucil restore commands: full daemon state backup (compressed tar.gz) and restore with integrity check; max 3 rolling backups retained.
run_tests MCP tool full implementation: executes tests for changed code via G8 test runner, returns results[], coverage_for_changed_lines, and untested_functions[].
Progressive startup integration test suite (tests/integration/test_progressive_startup.rs): verifies MCP server responds within 2s, priority file indexed within 30s, full index complete within 5min.
Install script (scripts/install.sh): one-line curl-pipe install downloads the correct binary for the current OS/arch, sets up .ucil/ in the home directory, and runs ucil init.
Claude Code plugin published: GitHub release tagged v0.1.0 contains plugin.json, .mcp.json, agents/, skills/, hooks/, and install instructions.
Public v0.1.0 release: README.md contains badges (build, license, stars), architecture screenshot, benchmark highlights, and quick-start in ≤5 steps.
Submission to awesome-mcp-servers, awesome-claude-mcp, and related MCP ecosystem lists completed; at least one merged submission PR link committed to docs/.
